functions.name: draw_frontend_widget
functions.description: Renders one shell-font front-end widget, including nine-slice border styles, slider-track strips, optional glow overlay, icon sprites, and the final text draw.

functions.name: initialize_frontend_sprite_button
functions.description: Constructs one sprite-backed front-end child control, including arrow-button animation ids, authored anchor, and immediate-vs-delayed click behavior.

functions.name: initialize_frontend_widget
functions.description: Constructs one shell-font front-end widget, seeds its border style, text scale, alignment, colors, and optional slider-child controls, then links it into the active widget list.

functions.name: layout_frontend_widget
functions.description: Recomputes one front-end widget's authored-space position, measured text bounds, and any attached slider-child offsets before the current frame draw.

functions.name: stack_widget_below
functions.description: Chains one shell-font front-end widget below another by copying the previous widget x anchor and adding the current gap plus the previous measured height.

functions.name: unhighlight_border
functions.description: Clears the active highlight state on one frontend border/widget and restores its non-highlighted visual bits.

functions.name: highlight_border
functions.description: Sets the active highlight state on one frontend border/widget so hover/selection visuals render in the highlighted style.

functions.name: update_frontend_widget_interaction
functions.description: Runs one front-end widget's per-frame interaction state, including hover easing, delayed click-bit dispatch, slider-arrow adjustments, bounds refresh, and draw.

functions.name: flush_row_event_display
functions.description: Fast-forwards the active gameplay row-event display by paying out any remaining row score awards, destroying its widget actors, and clearing the owning controller state.

functions.name: initialize_completion_screen
functions.description: Builds the Delivery Complete screen, seeds its package-count and bonus summary widgets, and lays out the continue prompt. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRCompletion::Init(int, bool)`.

functions.name: update_row_event_display
functions.description: Runs the gameplay row-event display state machine, including staged score payout, widget show or hide transitions, and the final handoff into `flush_row_event_display`.

functions.name: register_parcel_delivery
functions.description: Records one delivered parcel on the gameplay row-event display controller, awards the per-parcel score event, and dispatches the all-parcels-delivered bonus when the final parcel arrives.

functions.name: destroy_completion_screen
functions.description: Tears down the active completion-screen widgets and restores the owning front-end state once the current exit choice resolves. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRCompletion::UnInit()`.

functions.name: initialize_exit_prompt
functions.description: Builds the shared Yes/No exit prompt used by the front-end completion return flow, including the `Do you really want to quit?` title and the per-owner button layout variants. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRExit::Init()`.

functions.name: update_completion_screen
functions.description: Runs the completion-screen state machine, including continue, replay, route-map return, and outbound-link actions. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRCompletion::AI()`.

functions.name: initialize_default_runtime_config
functions.description: Seeds the 0xc4-byte SnailMail.cfg blob with default sound volume, music volume, fullscreen, progression, and tuning values before any file overlay is loaded.

functions.name: handle_game_window_activate
functions.description: Runtime reactivation helper for the main window. It clears the inactive flag, reapplies fullscreen mode when configured, refreshes the frame timestamp, shows and focuses the game window, and clears the mouse button latch bytes after WM_ACTIVATE enters the active state.

functions.name: show_and_focus_game_window
functions.description: Startup-only helper that shows the game window, brings it to the foreground, sets focus and active window state, refreshes the frame timestamp, and clears the mouse button latch bytes before the main loop begins.

functions.name: handle_game_window_deactivate
functions.description: Runtime deactivation helper for the main window. When the game is active in fullscreen mode, it marks the window inactive, minimizes it, and clears the pending quit flag before returning.

functions.name: game_window_proc
functions.description: Primary Win32 window procedure for the game's main window. It handles activation and minimize transitions, ESC-to-quit, left and right mouse button latches, and mouse-wheel delta capture before delegating the rest to DefWindowProcA.

functions.name: consume_mouse_wheel_delta
functions.description: Returns the latched mouse-wheel delta for the requested slot and clears it back to zero.

functions.name: read_left_mouse_button_state
functions.description: Clears the companion left-button latch byte for the requested slot and returns the current left mouse button state byte.

functions.name: read_right_mouse_button_state
functions.description: Clears the companion right-button latch byte for the requested slot and returns the current right mouse button state byte.

functions.name: snapshot_current_display_mode
functions.description: Reads the current desktop display mode into the shared `DEVMODEA` buffer at `data_4df868` before fullscreen or pointer-scaling setup.

functions.name: restore_desktop_display_mode
functions.description: Restores the desktop display mode with ChangeDisplaySettingsA(nullptr, 0) and makes the OS cursor visible again. The startup loop and deactivate path use it when leaving fullscreen presentation.

functions.name: probe_display_mode_count
functions.description: Walks `EnumDisplaySettingsA` until it fails and returns the terminal result, which the startup path uses while gathering desktop display capabilities.

functions.name: read_current_display_resolution
functions.description: Reads the current desktop width and height into the supplied outputs, falling back to `640x480` if `EnumDisplaySettingsA` fails.

functions.name: noop_runtime_ai
functions.description: Shared empty per-frame update stub reused by runtime object types that do not need standalone AI work. Cross-port Android symbol matches tie this address to multiple `::AI()` slots including camera, weapon, and ball-style objects.

functions.name: construct_game_runtime
functions.description: Allocates the `cRGame` root object, seeds its embedded managers and callback tables, initializes the runtime pools, and publishes the live global game pointer at `data_4df904`.

functions.name: initialize_garbage_hazard
functions.description: Constructs one garbage-hazard runtime object by running the shared body constructor and installing the cRSubGarbage vtable. `initialize_runtime_pools_and_path_template_bank` uses it to seed the 50-slot garbage pool that later feeds `spawn_track_garbage_hazard`.

functions.name: initialize_path_template_record_pair
functions.description: Constructs both 0x18-byte record halves for one paired path-template object and installs the shared vtable.

functions.name: initialize_active_bod
functions.description: Initializes one shared active-bod runtime header by clearing its list links and installing the common subtype callback table at `data_497338`.

functions.name: initialize_salt_hazard_runtime
functions.description: Constructs one salt-hazard runtime slot by running the shared body constructor and installing the salt-hazard vtable. `initialize_runtime_pools_and_path_template_bank` uses it to seed the 40-slot array later reset by `initialize_salt_hazard_pool`.

functions.name: initialize_fringe_object
functions.description: Constructs one 0x38-byte fringe render object by clearing the shared object base and installing the fringe-object vtable. `initialize_runtime_pools_and_path_template_bank` uses it to seed the 7000-entry fringe-manager pool later consumed by `allocate_fringe_object`.

functions.name: initialize_active_landscape_entry
functions.description: Constructs one active landscape-entry runtime record by clearing its shared body-list links and installing the landscape-entry update selector. `initialize_runtime_pools_and_path_template_bank` uses it to seed the 10-slot pool later driven by `activate_landscape_entry`.

functions.name: initialize_track_parcel_runtime
functions.description: Constructs one gameplay parcel runtime slot by clearing the shared body object and installing the parcel-slot vtable. `initialize_runtime_pools_and_path_template_bank` uses it to seed the 50-slot array at `game->track_parcels`.

functions.name: abort_startup_with_3d_error
functions.description: Shows the native 3D-accelerator startup error dialog, terminates the shell process, and returns through the shared `cRBod` constructor on the static sentinel object.

functions.name: initialize_bod
functions.description: Constructs one `cRBod` record by clearing its shared list links, installing the body update selector, and incrementing the global Bod-count ledger used by `construct_game_runtime`.

functions.name: load_galaxy_layout
functions.description: Loads `Galaxy/_Galaxy.txt`, rescales the authored galaxy-point tables, and populates the runtime galaxy route records with star counts, authored positions, and placeholder level labels.

functions.name: destroy_galaxy
functions.description: Tears down the active Star Map galaxy-screen widgets before replay launch, route handoff, or return to the owning front-end state. Cross-port Android symbols match this helper to `cRGalaxy::UnInit()`.

functions.name: initialize_galaxy
functions.description: Builds the Star Map galaxy screen, seeds its selected route from SnailMail.cfg, and wires the Deliver/Play, replay, and back controls. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRGalaxy::Init()`.

functions.name: update_galaxy
functions.description: Runs the Star Map galaxy-screen state machine, including route selection, replay launch, and Deliver/Play actions through the shared level-path handoff. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRGalaxy::AI()`.

functions.name: close_galaxy_route
functions.description: Closes the currently open Star Map route card, clears its active replay or play controls, and resets the open-route selection before another route is opened. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRGalaxy::BoxOff()`.

functions.name: open_galaxy_route
functions.description: Opens one selected Star Map route card, loads its preview label and widget stack, and toggles the replay-only controls for locked or cleared routes. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRGalaxy::Open(int)`.

functions.name: noop_runtime_callback
functions.description: Shared no-op callback used as the first slot in the root frontend/runtime update table at `data_4972d8`.

functions.name: initialize_frontend_overlay_color_lerp
functions.description: Seeds the secondary full-screen RGBA overlay interpolator stored at `data_4df904 + 0x2a8`.

functions.name: draw_frontend_overlay_color_lerp
functions.description: Advances and draws the secondary full-screen RGBA overlay interpolator stored at `data_4df904 + 0x2a8`.

functions.name: begin_frontend_fade_out
functions.description: Requests the shared front-end black transition overlay to fade from clear to full black and hold before a screen handoff.

functions.name: begin_frontend_fade_in
functions.description: Seeds the shared front-end black transition overlay at full black so the next screen fades in over 18 updates.

functions.name: update_frontend_transition_overlay
functions.description: Updates and draws the shared full-screen black front-end transition overlay, including fade-in, fade-out, three-tick black hold, and the handoff sentinel state.

functions.name: update_frontend_state_machine
functions.description: Dispatches the active front-end screen state, including menu, options, route map, intro, credits, high scores, gameplay handoff, the 26/27/28 subgame bridge states, and the post-run Thanks For Playing owner at states 29/30.

functions.name: set_backdrop_progress_fraction
functions.description: Stores one normalized progress fraction on the active backdrop controller. Windows `update_subgoldy` drives it from the current track-row payload ratio, and `initialize_backdrop` clears the same field at +0x6c8.

functions.name: set_backdrop_distort
functions.description: Seeds the shared backdrop distortion grid and its per-cell phase, speed, and amplitude terms from the script-side Distort scalar. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRBackdrop::SetDistort(float)`.

functions.name: change_backdrop
functions.description: Binds one parsed background script entry into the shared backdrop renderer, including its texture handles, Distort scalar, and split-vs-single draw mode. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRBackdrop::Change(cRLandscape*, bool)`.

functions.name: change_backdrop_real
functions.description: Commits the pending backdrop selection, refreshes the distortion grid when the world index changes, and copies the current split-vs-single draw flags into the active renderer state. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRBackdrop::ChangeReal()`.

functions.name: initialize_backdrop
functions.description: Initializes the shared backdrop renderer, seeds its texture-transition state, and primes its distortion grid. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRBackdrop::Init(int)`.

functions.name: set_backdrop_texture_target
functions.description: Captures the current and next backdrop texture ids and resets the internal crossfade counters inside the shared backdrop renderer.

functions.name: draw_split_backdrop
functions.description: Queues the split `_A` / `_B` backdrop textures directly into the sprite renderer when a background script points at a split menu or splash layout.

functions.name: render_backdrop
functions.description: Draws the single-texture 320x240 backdrop crop through the animated distortion grid. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRBackdrop::Render()`.

functions.name: update_backdrop
functions.description: Advances the shared backdrop renderer, updates its distortion grid, and dispatches the split or warped draw path. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRBackdrop::AI()`.

functions.name: initialize_game_window_and_input
functions.description: Registers the SnailMail window class, applies the recovered 4:3 resolution presets, falls back to a 640x480 windowed client, and initializes keyboard, controller, and mouse input.

functions.name: submit_sprite_draw_batches
functions.description: Walks the active sprite-texture batches for one scene, binds each texture ref, and submits the prepared sprite primitive streams into the renderer.

functions.name: draw_textured_quad_immediate
functions.description: Builds one immediate 2D textured quad in the transient UI vertex buffer, supporting explicit corner coordinates, axis-aligned size, or centered rotation before submitting the quad as a six-index primitive.

functions.name: configure_sprite_render_state
functions.description: Chooses the sprite render-state preset from the sprite draw-mode lane at `+0x28` before one sprite quad is submitted.

functions.name: draw_sprite_quad
functions.description: Builds and submits one textured sprite quad from the sprite color, scale, angle, and stretch lanes. The star-field pass at `initialize_star_field` and `update_star_positions` reaches this helper through the shared sprite renderer.

functions.name: get_or_append_object_texture_group_vertex
functions.description: Returns the existing grouped-render vertex matching one object vertex index plus UVs, or appends a new grouped vertex into the temporary texture-group build buffer when no match exists.

functions.name: build_object_texture_group_buffers
functions.description: Allocates one built object's texture-group tables, expands each grouped face run into triangle indices, and builds the matching Direct3D vertex and index buffers for grouped rendering.

functions.name: set_fullscreen_mode
functions.description: Switches the renderer's display mode between windowed (`0`) and fullscreen (`1`) through the shared device wrapper, updates the saved window rectangle bookkeeping, and recenters the cursor after the mode change.

functions.name: update_golb_ai
functions.description: Advances one live Golb shot actor, including straight-flight or path-follow motion, collision transitions, burst or smoke aftermath, and the final cleanup path once it passes the player or leaves the valid band.

functions.name: create_golb
functions.description: Initializes one Golb shot actor from the player's current movement_flags family and emitter slot, choosing the matching spawn anchor, velocity lane, render owner, and any path-follow state before the actor enters `update_golb_ai`.

functions.name: spawn_golb_trail_sprite
functions.description: Allocates one short-lived trail sprite for the straight-flight Golb family at the supplied world position. `update_golb_ai` emits it in a short three-point chain behind the projectile each tick.

functions.name: spawn_golb_impact_sprite
functions.description: Allocates the short-lived impact sprite used when a Golb shot is killed or reaches a terminal collision point, seeded from the supplied world position.

functions.name: initialize_challenge_setup_screen
functions.description: Builds the mode-1 front-end setup screen with Select Difficulty, Select Speed, Play, Watch Replay, and Back controls before challenge gameplay starts.

functions.name: destroy_challenge_setup_screen
functions.description: Tears down the mode-1 front-end setup screen widgets, including the optional replay control and the difficulty or speed selectors.

functions.name: update_challenge_setup_screen
functions.description: Runs the mode-1 front-end setup screen, including difficulty and speed changes, replay launch, Play, and Back handoff into `update_subgame` state 1.

functions.name: initialize_help
functions.description: Starts MAINMENU.OGG, loads Help.txt into the shared backdrop renderer, and allocates the single Back control. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRHelp::Init()`.

functions.name: destroy_help
functions.description: Clears the Help-screen shell-font scene before control returns to the owning front-end state.

functions.name: update_help
functions.description: Handles the Help-screen Back action, tears down the shell-font scene, and returns control to the New Game front-end flow via state `2`. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRHelp::AI()`.

functions.name: initialize_high_score_screen
functions.description: Builds the postal or challenge high-score screen, optionally enables inline name entry for a pending rank, and lays out the per-row replay or submit controls. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRHighScore::Init(int, int)`.

functions.name: destroy_high_score_screen
functions.description: Tears down the active high-score table widgets, preserves the selected bank index, and resets the shell-font scene before control returns to the owning front-end state. Cross-port Android symbols match this helper to `cRHighScore::UnInit()`.

functions.name: update_high_score_screen
functions.description: Runs the high-score screen state machine, including replay launch, bank toggling, inline name submission, and back or exit actions. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRHighScore::AI()`.

functions.name: initialize_high_score_tables
functions.description: Seeds the postal, challenge, and completion high-score banks with blank cRSubHighScore records before ScoreA/B/C overlays are loaded.

functions.name: add_arcade_high_score
functions.description: Attempts to insert one completed postal or arcade run into the ordered top-10 bank, then arms the high-score entry screen when the run places. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRSubHighScore::AddArcade(int)`.

functions.name: add_survival_high_score
functions.description: Attempts to insert one completed challenge or survival run into the ordered top-10 bank, mirrors the pending entry into the challenge bank overlay, and arms the high-score entry screen when the run places. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRSubHighScore::AddSurvival()`.

functions.name: add_time_trial_high_score
functions.description: Records one per-route time-trial result into the completion bank, replacing the stored entry only when the new time beats the existing route record. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRSubHighScore::AddTimeTrial(int, bool)`.

functions.name: initialize_high_score_entry
functions.description: Clears one cRSubHighScore record, seeds its score-mode metadata, and copies the current player-name buffer into the entry name field.

functions.name: commit_high_score_entry_into_top_ten
functions.description: Copies the edited scratch high-score entry into the selected insertion row and shifts the remaining top-10 rows down.

functions.name: exit_high_score_screen
functions.description: Leaves the high-score screen and resumes the front-end state that owns the postal or challenge table. Cross-port Android symbols match this helper to `cRHighScore::Exit()`.

functions.name: initialize_new_game_menu
functions.description: Builds the New Game submenu, including Tutorial, Postal Mode, Time Trial, Challenge Mode, Help, and Back, after reloading the shared menu backdrop and shell-font scene.

functions.name: update_new_game_menu
functions.description: Runs the New Game menu controller, dispatching Tutorial, Postal Mode, Time Trial, Challenge Mode, Help, and Back through the shared front-end state machine.

functions.name: load_landscape_script_by_name
functions.description: Loads or reuses one `Backgrounds/<name>` landscape script, parses its `ID`, `Fog`, `Picture`, `Landscape`, and `Distort` fields, registers the referenced backdrop textures, and returns the cached landscape index.

functions.name: activate_landscape_entry
functions.description: Binds one cached landscape entry into the shared backdrop manager, applies its texture or model handles, chooses the random variant flag, and copies its fog color into the global render state.

functions.name: clear_active_landscape_entries
functions.description: Clears the active 10-slot landscape-entry runtime pool by unlinking every live entry from the shared body list and zeroing its startup-delay lane. `destroy_subgame` uses it when the current landscape set is torn down.

functions.name: update_active_landscape_entry
functions.description: Advances one active landscape-entry runtime by counting down its activation delay, shifting its lateral offset, and toggling its shared visibility bit as it moves into or out of the backdrop camera band.

functions.name: initialize_loading_screen
functions.description: Loads the loading-screen textures, seeds the full-screen quad and progress-bar quad, and resets the loader progress counters.

functions.name: destroy_loading_screen
functions.description: Tears down the loading-screen textures and preserves the last measured loading budget for the next loader pass.

functions.name: update_loading_screen
functions.description: Advances the normalized loader percentage, draws the loading background, and clips the LoadingBarOn fill quad into the recovered 192x289 to 448x321 window.

functions.name: initialize_intro_screen
functions.description: Loads SpaceRed plus INTROTEXT.OGG, parses the intro or credits script into centered text and image renderables on the tilted crawl plane, and derives the shared scroll delta from Duration. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRIntro::Init()`.

functions.name: destroy_intro_screen
functions.description: Unlinks each intro or credits crawl renderable from the active body lists and returns it to the shared free list before the text screen exits. Cross-port Android symbols match this helper to `cRIntro::UnInit()`.

functions.name: update_intro_screen
functions.description: Advances the normalized intro or credits crawl, tears down its renderables once the scroll completes, and exits early on skip input. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRIntro::AI()`.

functions.name: destroy_main_menu
functions.description: Clears the active menu shell-font scene and tears down the current main-menu widget set before control leaves the front-end. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match the owning flow to `cRMainMenu::UnInit()`.

functions.name: initialize_main_menu
functions.description: Starts MAINMENU.OGG, loads Menubg.txt into the shared backdrop renderer, seeds the front-end state to menu mode, and allocates the New Game, High Scores, Options, Credits, and Exit widgets. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRMainMenu::Init()`.

functions.name: update_main_menu
functions.description: Handles main-menu selection, front-end screen transitions, and the tutorial launch handoff. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRMainMenu::AI()`.

functions.name: sort_object_faces_by_texture_group
functions.description: Sorts one built object's 0x30-byte face records by the texture-group key at `face + 0xc` so `calc_object_texture_groups` can collapse contiguous runs.

functions.name: initialize_textured_backdrop_quad
functions.description: Builds one shared 4-vertex textured backdrop quad with full-range UVs for the `Objects/Univers*` lane.

functions.name: raise_backdrop_quad_edge_pair
functions.description: Raises one of the two adjacent backdrop-quad edge pairs by 0.5 units using the small orientation tables consumed by the `Objects/Univers` variants.

functions.name: initialize_backdrop_slice_quad
functions.description: Builds one shared 4-vertex textured backdrop quad using a horizontal 1/8 UV slice selected by the panel index for the `Objects/World00*` lane.

functions.name: initialize_backdrop_corner_quad
functions.description: Builds one sliced backdrop quad and offsets the selected edge pair by 0.3 units using the four-entry corner selector table.

functions.name: sample_smtrack_heightmap
functions.description: Samples one source texture into the active SMTrack float-height grid by averaging the source RGB channels, applying the optional cubic curve, and scaling the result into the destination height lane.

functions.name: initialize_backdrop_tile_quad
functions.description: Builds one oriented textured backdrop tile quad by choosing one of the eight UV-orientation cases and offsetting the local tile with the supplied row and column selectors.

functions.name: initialize_options
functions.description: Constructs the Options screen widgets, seeds them from SnailMail.cfg, and links the fullscreen, sounds, music, and back controls. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cROptions::Init()`.

functions.name: destroy_options
functions.description: Tears down the Options-screen widgets and writes the current SnailMail.cfg blob back to disk. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cROptions::UnInit()`.

functions.name: update_options
functions.description: Runs the Options-screen state machine, including fullscreen toggles, slider updates, audio-volume refresh, save, and return to the owning front-end state. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cROptions::AI()`.

functions.name: apply_audio_config_volumes
functions.description: Pushes the saved sound and music scalar fields from SnailMail.cfg into the audio engine.

functions.name: begin_track_attachment_follow_state
functions.description: Starts one live attachment-follow session from the selected runtime attachment cell by marking the follow struct active, storing the template and owner cell, zeroing the segment index, seeding progress from `world_z - cell_anchor_z`, seeding local height from `world_y - 0.49`, caching the player pointer, and copying the row payload into template `+0x98`. Windows `cdb` confirmed shipped `ARCADE007` HalfPipe entries hit this helper with entry progress around `0.03595` and near-zero entry height.

functions.name: update_track_attachment_follow_state
functions.description: Advances one live attachment-follow session along the current template, updates segment, progress, and local height, writes the interpolated output transform back into the follow struct, and returns a small mode code consumed by `update_subgoldy`. Windows `cdb` confirmed `ARCADE007` produces mid-follow samples with local height `-0.49f`, and the case-1 or case-3 return path feeds one direct fall-state handoff inside `update_subgoldy`.

functions.name: mirror_path_template_pair_x
functions.description: Clones one path-template pair, negates X-space samples, and flips strip winding for the mirrored half.

functions.name: find_segment_path_index_by_name
functions.description: Linearly searches the hardcoded 51-entry Path= name table and returns the matching index or -1.

functions.name: initialize_kind42_path_template_pair
functions.description: Builds one nonlinear kind-42 attachment template branch. The constructor seeds sample_count=66, writes the special per-sample scalar at +0xa0, and emits a generated strip mesh through the shared kind-42 transform helper. Android's cRPath::BuildHalfPipe is a strong structural match, but the exact Windows public-path mapping remains open.

functions.name: compute_kind42_attachment_transform
functions.description: Computes the nonlinear temporary transform used by attachment kind 42 during projection, live follow, and constructor-side strip generation. Android's cRPath::HalfPipePos is a strong structural match, but the exact Windows public-path naming for this family remains open.

functions.name: finalize_path_template_record
functions.description: Finalizes one generated path-template record after constructor or mirror edits by recomputing derived per-sample lengths and updating the nested strip-mesh state.

functions.name: try_enter_track_attachment_from_swept_motion
functions.description: Scans the authored runtime attachment samples against the player's swept motion box, seeds the shared scratch follow-state block when a candidate hit survives the local bounds tests, and immediately runs one follow update to validate the candidate. Windows `cdb` confirmed shipped `ARCADE007` HalfPipe play reaches the live follow-state path driven by this entry family.

functions.name: initialize_bod_base
functions.description: Initializes the shared bod header and default state: noop runtime AI callback, default flags, white color block, cleared linkage fields, and the global bod counter increment.

functions.name: advance_frame_sequence
functions.description: Advances one frame-sequence block by its fractional step, handling wrap, restart, reverse, and ping-pong flags before publishing the current frame payload pointer.

functions.name: load_archive_index
functions.description: Decodes and parses the SnailMail.dat archive index into lookup records for archive-backed file loading.

functions.name: report_errorf
functions.description: Formats and emits one fatal-style engine error message with a varargs payload. Runtime and content-loading paths use it for hard failures such as missing hotspot textures, parcel-pool exhaustion, and invalid track segment lengths.

functions.name: set_input_controller_slot0_button_axes
functions.description: Stores the keyboard-derived button mask and X/Y axis floats into input-controller slot 0, overwriting its shared digital and analog lanes before later pointer data is merged in.

functions.name: update_input_controller_slot_button_axes
functions.description: Updates one global input-controller slot from a button mask and two analog axes. Slot 0 merges button bits and only fills empty analog lanes, while nonzero slots overwrite their lane directly and derive the slot's centered authored-X value from the X axis with the native 0.15 deadzone.

functions.name: copy_active_input_controller_state
functions.description: Copies one of the two global input-controller slots into the caller-owned runtime buffer, including button flags, normalized pointer coordinates, and the per-slot authored cursor lanes used by gameplay input.

functions.name: update_input_controller_pointer_region
functions.description: Refreshes one global input-controller slot from a screen-space pointer region: stores the active bounds, optionally warps the pixel cursor into that region, updates normalized authored 640x480 coordinates, and latches the per-slot button bits.

functions.name: set_input_controller_pointer_authored_xy
functions.description: Seeds one global input-controller slot to an authored 640x480 pointer position, updates its normalized coordinates from the stored region bounds, and warps the backing OS cursor through click_mouse_screen.

functions.name: read_pressed_text_input_key_code
functions.description: Maps newly pressed keyboard scancodes into the shared text-input byte codes used by frontend widgets, cheat entry, intro skip, and subgame ESC handling: ASCII for letters/digits/punctuation plus the small control-code set for edit and navigation keys.

functions.name: read_repeating_text_input_key_code
functions.description: Maps held keyboard scancodes into the same text-input byte codes as read_pressed_text_input_key_code, but with the native repeat delay/rate state so border_input_text can autorepeat held edits and cursor moves.

functions.name: cache_music_file
functions.description: Ensures one `music/*.ogg` payload is resident in the shared music-file cache, loading it from the archive or filesystem on first use and reusing the cached bytes afterward.

functions.name: play_sound_effect_backend
functions.description: Shared SFX backend wrapper that plays one effect id with an explicit gain scale plus optional extra playback parameters before forwarding into the lower sound-bank calls.

functions.name: play_voice_backend
functions.description: Shared voice backend wrapper that plays one voice sample id with an explicit gain scale plus the same optional extra playback parameters used by the SFX backend.

functions.name: register_sound_sample
functions.description: Registers one voice or sound-bank sample path into the shared 256-entry runtime sound table, loading bytes from the archive on first use and returning the assigned sample index.

functions.name: initialize_track_render_cache_manager
functions.description: Initializes the fixed 143x5 track-render cache bod grid plus its shared GDX cache vertex/index buffers and per-cache texture-group scratch arrays.

functions.name: build_track_render_caches
functions.description: Builds the Floor, Slide, Warn, Ramp, and Fringe track render caches after runtime track normalization.

functions.name: update_active_bod
functions.description: Implements the default active-bod subtype callback by recycling a live bod to the free list once its world-z plus the native 24-unit buffer falls behind the current subgame cutoff.

functions.name: remove_track_render_cache_bods
functions.description: Unlinks the fixed 143x5 track-render cache bod grid from the shared active-bod list during subgame teardown.

functions.name: initialize_thanks_for_playing_screen
functions.description: Initializes the post-run Thanks For Playing owner, including the Splash backdrop, INTROTEXT.OGG, and the first shell-font message widget before state 30 begins updating it.

functions.name: update_thanks_for_playing_screen
functions.description: Runs the post-run Thanks For Playing message sequence, including the Challenge Mode and Time Trial prompts, Click to Continue, and the fade-out handoff back through the saved front-end return state.

functions.name: destroy_star_field
functions.description: Destroys the live star-field sprite pool, tears down its per-entry records, and returns the controller to state 0.

functions.name: open_star_field
functions.description: Allocates the persistent star-field controller storage and, when the global star-pass flag is enabled, immediately initializes one sprite-based streak entry per requested slot.

functions.name: initialize_star_field
functions.description: Builds the live star-field streak sprites: allocates one sprite per entry, seeds camera-relative positions, randomizes direction and speed, tints them pale blue-white, and primes the per-streak length or fade fields.

functions.name: hide_star_field
functions.description: Clears bit 0x40 on every live star-field sprite, hiding the background star pass without destroying its controller state.

functions.name: unhide_star_field
functions.description: Sets bit 0x40 on every live star-field sprite, re-enabling the background streak pass without rebuilding the controller.

functions.name: update_star_field
functions.description: Runs the star-field controller state machine, fading the streak sprites in or out around the global star-pass flag and dispatching the per-frame camera-relative position update.

functions.name: update_star_positions
functions.description: Advances each star-field streak along its cached direction, resets wrapped entries against the current camera origin, and scales the visible sprite trail by both per-streak speed and the controller fade factor.

functions.name: build_track_fringe_objects
functions.description: Allocates directional fringe objects around runtime cells for the post-build renderer, using the shared fringe-manager pool at `data_4df904 + 0x3d01d4`.

functions.name: merge_track_tile_runs
functions.description: Collapses repeated horizontal tile runs into longer strip variants where the renderer allows it.

functions.name: mark_track_warning_zones
functions.description: Marks warning-zone footprints around hazard-bearing runtime tiles before the fringe-object and cache builders run. Cross-port Android symbols match this pass to `cRSubGame::WarnTrack()`.

functions.name: harmonize_center_lane_floor_slide_variants
functions.description: Normalizes center-lane floor and slide seam variants after the main edge pass. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this pass to `cRSubGame::SlideSmoothTrack()`.

functions.name: select_track_tile_edge_variants
functions.description: Computes neighbor masks and chooses edge or corner variants for special runtime tile classes. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this pass to `cRSubGame::SmoothTrack()`.

functions.name: populate_runtime_track_cells_from_segments
functions.description: Copies authored segment rows into the generated runtime grid, including Random and Length handling, and seeds Goldy's visible life stock to 3 before `initialize_subgoldy` runs.

functions.name: initialize_subgame
functions.description: Initializes the active gameplay subgame state, including the continuation selector at `+0x1270fc8`, per-mode HUD widgets, replay-backed startup, and the first galaxy, challenge-setup, or gameplay handoff. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRSubGame::Init()`.

functions.name: rebuild_track_runtime_from_segments
functions.description: Normalizes the generated runtime grid, copies authored metadata into runtime cells, and runs the post-build render passes.

functions.name: build_subgame_level
functions.description: Builds the active subgame course, including level-file load, runtime track rebuild, mode-specific HUD setup, backdrop selection, and the first gameplay object lists. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRSubGame::BuildLevel()`.

functions.name: complete_subgame
functions.description: Snapshots the just-finished run into the subgame result bank, increments the per-run completion counter, and dispatches the delivery, galaxy, or cutscene handoff for the finished course. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRSubGame::Complete(bool)`.

functions.name: destroy_subgame
functions.description: Tears down the active gameplay subgame state, including runtime lists, HUD widgets, and the current mode handoff state before control returns to the front-end. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRSubGame::UnInit()`.

functions.name: update_subgame
functions.description: Runs the main gameplay subgame state machine, including the continuation-controlled galaxy or challenge-setup handoff, replay-backed level start or restart, pause and quit flow, HUD timing, camera updates, and the active gameplay tick. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRSubGame::AI()`.

functions.name: refresh_fringe_object_draw_list
functions.description: Copies the current track skirt tint into one fringe object and, once its world-z falls behind the live fringe threshold, unlinks the object from the active list and requeues it onto the shared draw-list bucket.

functions.name: destroy_wall2_ambient_hazard
functions.description: Unlinks one live `Wall2` ambient-hazard actor and its attached body objects from the shared intrusive lists before the slot returns to the inactive pool.

functions.name: maybe_spawn_wall2_ambient_hazard
functions.description: Runs the current best static `Wall2` ambient-hazard spawn gate from one runtime row cell: checks the row or tile conditions, rolls the random gate, derives a spawn point and velocity, and routes the request into the 20-slot pool at `game + 0x356b00`.

functions.name: update_player_movement_flags
functions.description: Maps the player movement-flag selector at +0x308 onto movement_flags and a per-state rate scalar at +0x2734, then refreshes the 0x2984 state machine when the mask changes.

functions.name: update_movement_flag_emitters
functions.description: Iterates the 12 Golb emitter slots at player+0x450 and seeds one, two, or three live shot actors based on the current movement_flags family.

functions.name: update_jetpack_gauge
functions.description: Advances the player jetpack countdown or warning controller at +0x2750: animates the near-empty meter, triggers the auto-shutoff branch when the current runtime cell carries flag 0x80, and clears the controller once the jetpack gauge drains. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols still name the owning class `cRDamageGuage::AI()`.

functions.name: initialize_jetpack_gauge
functions.description: Initializes the player jetpack countdown or warning controller at +0x2750, wiring the live Game pointer and warning anchor used by `update_jetpack_gauge`. Cross-port Android symbols still name the owning class `cRDamageGuage::Init()`.

functions.name: arm_jetpack_gauge
functions.description: Arms the player jetpack countdown controller at +0x2750 from the pickup path: sets the active flag, zeros progress and wobble offsets, plays the jetpack start cue, and seeds the warning visuals.

functions.name: initialize_subgoldy
functions.description: Initializes the live Goldy actor for a new run: resets movement and follow state, seeds the animation managers and cutscene block, initializes the jetpack gauge and cameraman, and wires the actor back to the active Game. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRSubGoldy::Init(int)`.

functions.name: show_subgoldy_lives
functions.description: Refreshes the nine-slot Goldy life display by showing the first `current_lives` widgets and hiding the rest. Windows `update_subgoldy` only calls it for gameplay mode 0, matching the mode-gated `cRSubGoldy::ShowLives()` helper in the Android and iOS ports.

functions.name: initialize_subgoldy_fall_state
functions.description: Seeds Goldy's post-follow fall state. If the active follow byte is still set, it copies the current attachment payload and cached carryover scalar into the post-follow slots at `+0x430` and `+0x42c`, clears `+0x384`, sets `+0x41d`, latches player z into `attachment_exit_anchor_z` at `+0x424`, and zeroes the exit-progress and gate bytes. Windows `cdb` confirmed this helper can also run after the active follow byte has already been cleared, so it is one real exit lane but not the only attachment-retirement path.

functions.name: play_movement_state_sound
functions.description: Chooses one of three movement sound families from movement_flags and attenuates playback while attachment-exit handling is active.

functions.name: update_subgoldy
functions.description: Main per-frame player or Goldy actor step. Updates attachment-follow state, samples current track cells, drives row events, dispatches pickups and completion handoff, emits movement flags and sounds, and advances the runtime track index. It contains four direct calls to `initialize_subgoldy_fall_state`, plus the post-exit progress loop that advances `attachment_exit_progress` by `attachment_exit_progress_step` and trips the `gate_a` and `gate_b` thresholds. Windows `cdb` confirmed those fall-helper lanes are real, but at least one clean level-complete path bypasses all four of them. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRSubGoldy::AI()`.

functions.name: mark_current_track_pair_with_payload
functions.description: Sets flag 0x40 on the two cached track cells at +0x98/+0x9c and writes the supplied scalar payload into each cell payload slot at +0x50.

functions.name: get_track_grid_cell_at_world_position
functions.description: Clamps world-space x and z into the 8-lane gameplay grid and returns the compact 0x54-byte runtime cell.

functions.name: get_track_runtime_cell_at_world_z
functions.description: Clamps world z into the generated row array and returns the current runtime cell.

functions.name: sample_track_floor_height_at_position
functions.description: Evaluates the runtime floor-height sampler for the current gameplay-grid cell, including ramp tiles and the special cell-stored height used by tile 0x16.

functions.name: spawn_track_health_pickup
functions.description: Allocates and seeds one live health pickup from the active runtime row state. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRSubGame::AddHealth(cRSubLoc*, cRSubGoldy*)`.

functions.name: spawn_track_jetpack_pickup
functions.description: Allocates and seeds one live jetpack pickup from the active runtime row state. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRSubGame::AddJetPack(cRSubLoc*, cRSubGoldy*)`.

functions.name: spawn_track_garbage_hazard
functions.description: Allocates and seeds one live garbage hazard from the active runtime row state. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRSubGame::AddGarbage(cRSubLoc*, cRSubGoldy*)`.

functions.name: spawn_slug_hazard
functions.description: Allocates and seeds one live slug hazard from the active runtime row state. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRSubGame::AddSlug(cRSubLoc*, cRSubGoldy*)`.

functions.name: spawn_track_ring_or_special_effect
functions.description: Dispatches authored Ring=* rows into the ring, explode, or slow particle effect families.

functions.name: initialize_ring_or_special_effect_particles
functions.description: Maps ring or special-effect modes onto the ParticleRing, ParticleExplode, and ParticleSlow sprite families.

functions.name: update_ring_or_special_effect_particle
functions.description: Advances one child sprite in the bullet-owned ring or special-effect halo, updating its circular phase around the parent projectile and refreshing the live effect position before optional star-shower emission.

functions.name: destroy_garbage_hazard
functions.description: Removes one live garbage hazard from the active body lists, kills its sprite, and unlinks it from the active garbage chain after collision or expiry. Cross-port Android symbols match this helper to `cRSubGarbage::Kill()`.

functions.name: update_garbage_hazard
functions.description: Advances one live garbage hazard through its hover, impact-burst, bounce, smoke, and teardown states. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRSubGarbage::AI()`.

functions.name: deserialize_compact_high_score_record
functions.description: Validates one compact ScoreA/B/C record checksum, copies its scalar fields, name, and replay samples into the expanded cRSubHighScore entry, and marks the entry active.

functions.name: serialize_compact_high_score_record
functions.description: Compacts one active cRSubHighScore entry into the ScoreA/B/C on-disk record format, including checksum, name, and variable replay payload arrays.

functions.name: add_subgoldy_score
functions.description: Adds one scored gameplay event onto Goldy's per-event buckets and total score, and awards a bonus life whenever the total crosses a 50,000-point threshold while the visible life stock is still below nine. Windows evidence matches the `cRSubGoldy::ScoreAdd(int,int)` shape directly.

functions.name: clear_subgoldy_score_buckets
functions.description: Clears the six per-type score buckets that `add_subgoldy_score` and `display_score_stats` read on the live subgame runner owner, without touching the separate total-score or life lanes.

functions.name: update_damage_gauge
functions.description: Advances the player contact-damage gauge controller at +0x3c4, smoothing its displayed fill, driving the warning or drain presentation, and rendering the sprite-backed gauge widget each gameplay tick.

functions.name: apply_damage_gauge_delta
functions.description: Applies one signed delta to the player contact-damage gauge controller at +0x3c4, clamps the live fill to [0,1], and triggers the matching sound or animation side effects. Windows collision callsites feed this helper with -0.5 health recovery, +0.04 garbage, +0.15 salt, +1.0 slug, and the smaller +0.02 `Wall2` ambient-hazard path.

functions.name: spawn_salt_hazard
functions.description: Allocates one live salt.x hazard slot from the 40-slot pool and seeds its runtime state.

functions.name: initialize_wall2_ambient_hazard_pool
functions.description: Clears the 20-slot `Wall2` ambient-hazard pool by zeroing each slot state at +0x80 before a level build.

functions.name: spawn_wall2_ambient_hazard
functions.description: Activates one slot from the 20-slot `Wall2` ambient-hazard pool: stores world position and velocity, seeds the arming step from `game->track_center_x`, links the slot into the shared intrusive-list machinery, and refreshes the nested render or body object.

functions.name: deactivate_salt_hazard
functions.description: Marks a salt hazard slot inactive after collision or after it leaves the supported path.

functions.name: update_salt_hazard
functions.description: Integrates one active salt hazard, tests track attachment intersection, and deactivates it after collision or exit.

functions.name: shoot_subgoldy
functions.description: Finds a free slot in the shared 20-shot pool, routes the request through `spawn_wall2_ambient_hazard`, and plays the positional player-fire cue from the supplied world position.

functions.name: advance_timer_counters
functions.description: Accumulates one generic timer record from a 60 Hz-scaled delta, rolls the frame counter at 60, and refreshes the derived hundredths and thousandths fields from the fractional remainder.

functions.name: update_fringe_object
functions.description: Advances one live fringe object's fade state from the shared track z thresholds, updates its alpha tint, and retires the object from the active list once it crosses the far cutoff.

functions.name: update_smtracks
functions.description: Advances the `cRSMTracks` manager's active frame sequence, copies the live replacement scalar into the selected SMTrack texture slot, and resamples the active source texture into the runtime height grid.

functions.name: initialize_subgoldy_resurrect
functions.description: Seeds Goldy's resurrect state machine by storing the respawn-or-final-loss selector, enabling the resurrect-active flag, and resetting the shared fade timer. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRSubGoldy::RessurectInit(int)`.

functions.name: update_subgoldy_resurrect
functions.description: Advances Goldy's resurrect or death-resolution state machine after `initialize_subgoldy_death`: starts the fade, decrements the visible life stock on the respawn branch, and only falls through to `complete_subgame` when the final-loss path wins. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRSubGoldy::RessurectAI()`.

functions.name: get_track_skirt_color
functions.description: Builds the shared RGBA skirt or fringe colour from the current track colour scalars and a fixed alpha, then copies it into one runtime row or fringe object. Cross-port Android symbols match this helper to `cRSubGame::GetSkirtColour(tColourSmall)`.

functions.name: initialize_cutscene
functions.description: Seeds and polls the player-side cutscene controller from the live player transform, the cached snail-hotspot transforms, and the completion handoff state; Windows `update_subgoldy` calls it every tick, and it dispatches `update_cutscene` whenever the controller is active. Cross-port Android symbols still match the helper family to `cRCutScene::Init()`.

functions.name: update_track_parcels
functions.description: Walks the 50-slot `game->track_parcels` runtime array and dispatches `update_track_parcel` on each active parcel slot.

functions.name: initialize_track_parcel_slots
functions.description: Clears the live state on the 50-slot gameplay parcel array and reseeds each slot's `game` pointer before any authored parcel placements are spawned for the current level.

functions.name: allocate_track_parcel_slot
functions.description: Scans the gameplay parcel array for the first inactive slot and returns it, logging `Too Many Parcels increase RPARCEL_MAXIMUM` if the 50-slot pool is exhausted.

functions.name: update_track_parcel
functions.description: Runs one live gameplay parcel slot through its bobbing, homing, and final delivery-arc states, then registers the delivered parcel on `game->row_event_display` before tearing down the sprite.

functions.name: load_frontend_level_by_mode_and_index
functions.description: Formats the mode-specific level filename for postal, challenge, time-trial, or tutorial front-end launches and then loads that level definition file.

functions.name: spawn_track_parcel
functions.description: Allocates one live gameplay parcel slot from `game->track_parcels`, seeds its world position, sprite, and parcel-owner pointer, and returns the activated runtime slot.

functions.name: initialize_parcel_set_bucket_pool
functions.description: Initializes the 0x800-entry positive-count parcel-set bucket pool at `data_6487e8`, clearing each 0x20c-byte entry with the shared gameplay slot initializer before `place_parcels_on_track` fills it from authored parcel requests.

functions.name: initialize_zero_parcel_bucket_pool
functions.description: Initializes the 0x800-entry zero-parcel fallback bucket pool at `data_53d190`, clearing each 0x20c-byte entry with the shared gameplay slot initializer before `place_parcels_on_track` uses it for `0`-parcel filler rows.

functions.name: place_parcels_on_track
functions.description: Places parcels for the current course and projects flagged parcel offsets onto generated track rows. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRSubGame::PlaceParcels()`.

functions.name: place_challenge_parcels_on_track
functions.description: Scans parcel-capable runtime rows whose copied parcel id is zero and places challenge parcels from that filtered set. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRSubGame::PlaceParcelsSurvival()`.

functions.name: project_position_onto_track_attachment
functions.description: Projects a world-space position onto an active track attachment template.

functions.name: dispatch_cutscene_animation
functions.description: Either queues one cutscene or presentation animation id onto the player-side channel or begins it immediately, resetting the live slot state when the immediate path wins.

functions.name: update_anim_manager
functions.description: Advances one queued presentation animation channel, applying loop, bounce, or clamp flags and starting the next queued entry when the current one finishes.

functions.name: initialize_invincible_shell
functions.description: Resets the runner-owned invincible-shell controller at `player + 0x4218` and restores the normal snail-skin state before gameplay starts.

functions.name: start_invincible_shell
functions.description: Arms the runner-owned invincible-shell controller when the native invincible bit becomes active, seeds its fade timers, raises the shared render bit, and plays `SFX2/INVINCIBLE.OGG`.

functions.name: update_invincible_shell
functions.description: Advances the runner-owned invincible-shell controller: fades the shell in while the invincible movement-flag bit stays active, keeps the shell spinning on the shared snail matrix, and fades back to the base skin once invincibility clears.

functions.name: handle_subgoldy_collisions
functions.description: Processes Goldy's live collision sweep against rings, parcels, health pickups, garbage, salt, slugs, and related runtime object pools, updating score and the contact-damage gauge side effects as each hit resolves. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRSubGoldy::Collision()`.

functions.name: set_snail_weapon
functions.description: Maps the requested player weapon state onto the three visual-state channels rooted at +0x2984 and refreshes the active weapon presentation.

functions.name: update_snail_skin
functions.description: Transforms the 19-entry local snail hotspot bank at `+0x16cc` into the live world-space hotspot bank at `+0x17b0`, using two cached matrices and feeding the cutscene anchor points later consumed by `update_cutscene`, including the authored `CameraSkidStop` and `CameraIntroTalk` hotspots.

functions.name: build_snail_hotspots
functions.description: Builds the 19-entry local snail hotspot bank from named hotpoint textures on the loaded snail model, including the authored `CameraSkidStop`, `CameraSlugDeath`, and `CameraIntroTalk` entries later transformed by `update_snail_skin` and consumed by the cutscene path.

functions.name: show_times_up_message
functions.description: Spawns the floating `Time's Up` gameplay message once the subgame timer hits its terminal threshold.

functions.name: update_snail_skin_transition
functions.description: Applies the currently selected snail-skin object to the shared player render owner and, when a timed skin change is armed, advances the transition progress until the swap completes.

functions.name: update_subgame_camera
functions.description: Runs the active subgame camera update, choosing between the live cameraman follow pose, cached camera states, and direct identity handoffs before interpolating the view matrix back into the shared camera. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to the outer `cRSubGame::CameraAI()` flow.

functions.name: initialize_cutscene_ai
functions.description: Constructs the embedded player-side cutscene AI/controller at `player + 0x42dc`, seeding its shared pointers and clearing the live cutscene state before `initialize_subgoldy` selects the startup leg.

functions.name: initialize_cameraman
functions.description: Initializes the follow-camera controller, wiring its shared player and global pointers, clearing the cached matrices, and seeding the default zoom used by `update_subgame_camera`. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRCameraman::Init()`.

functions.name: update_cameraman
functions.description: Builds the live follow-camera pose from player transform, attachment state, motion scalars, and backdrop zoom before handing the matrix back to `update_subgame_camera`. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRCameraman::AI()`.

functions.name: update_cutscene
functions.description: Advances the player-side cutscene controller, consuming the live snail hotspot bank as the intro/completion/death camera anchor source, including the authored `CameraSkidStop -> CameraIntroTalk` blend path, and handling the handoff into `initialize_completion_screen`. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRCutScene::AI()`.

functions.name: initialize_subgoldy_death
functions.description: Chooses Goldy's death outcome from the current gameplay mode and visible life stock, then enters the resurrect state machine in either respawn or final-loss mode. Cross-port Android and iOS symbols match this helper to `cRSubGoldy::DeathInit()`.

functions.name: initialize_fringe_manager
functions.description: Resets the shared fringe-manager pool counter at `data_4df904 + 0x3d01d4` before one track rebuild emits directional fringe objects. Cross-port Android symbols match this helper to `cRFringeManager::Init()`.

functions.name: allocate_fringe_object
functions.description: Allocates the next 0x38-byte object from the shared 7000-entry fringe-manager pool, or raises the `Too many Fringes` error if the pool overflows. Cross-port Android symbols match this helper to `cRFringeManager::GetFringe()`.

functions.name: uninit_nuke
functions.description: Kills the 25 sprite slots owned by the collision ring-effect controller and clears its active state. Cross-port Android symbols match this helper to `cRNuke::UnInit()`.

functions.name: initialize_nuke
functions.description: Spawns the 25-sprite ring effect used by ring/special-effect collision kinds `2` and `6`, seeds its orbit height and phase step from the owning player and global tick delta, and immediately dispatches the first update. Cross-port Android symbols match this helper to `cRNuke::Init()`.

functions.name: update_nuke
functions.description: Advances the 25-sprite collision ring effect by orbiting its sprite slots around the owner at radius `7.0` while the owning timer remains active. Cross-port Android symbols match this helper to `cRNuke::AI()`.

functions.name: recycle_bod_to_free_list
functions.description: Unlinks one active BOD from its current list membership and moves it back onto the shared free-list head, erroring if the source node is not currently marked as live-list owned.

functions.name: load_builtin_segment_definitions
functions.description: Copies the shipped built-in segment definitions from the static table at `0x4a63d0` into the runtime segment catalog, including names like `Start`, `Finish`, and `Filler` plus their eight authored row strings and metadata lanes.

functions.name: load_segment_definitions
functions.description: Parses SEGMENTS/*.TXT into the segment catalog and per-row metadata arrays.

functions.name: load_level_definitions
functions.description: Composes level descriptors and segment sequences from LEVELS/*.TXT.

functions.name: kill_tip_widgets
functions.description: Kills the live border widgets owned by one gameplay tip slot, including the main tip body and any optional OK or Disable buttons, then clears the tip's active flag.

functions.name: initialize_tip
functions.description: Builds one gameplay tip slot, including the main border widget, optional OK or Disable buttons, and the timed auto-dismiss rate for transient tips.

functions.name: update_tip
functions.description: Runs one gameplay tip slot, handling hide or show state, button-click consumption, timed expiry, and the final teardown or UI-state restore path.

functions.name: enqueue_tip_message
functions.description: Allocates one of the three small gameplay tip-message slots, seeds it with the requested text payload, and warns if all slots are already occupied.

functions.name: initialize_bass_audio_backend
functions.description: Extracts Bass.dll from game data into tBass.dll, loads the BASS 2.0 exports dynamically, and initializes the audio backend.

functions.name: uninitialize_bass_audio_backend
functions.description: Stops the global BASS backend, frees the loaded `Bass.dll` state, unloads the temporary DLL, and deletes the extracted `tBass.dll` copy.

functions.name: ensure_music_stream_from_path
functions.description: Ensures the requested music path is the active BASS stream, skipping a restart when the cached path already matches and otherwise stopping any previous stream before opening and playing the new path.

functions.name: prepare_music_stream_reload_if_path_changed
functions.description: Checks whether the requested music path differs from the cached active path and, when it does, stops the current music stream so `cache_music_file` can reload it from archive bytes.

functions.name: play_music_stream_from_bytes
functions.description: Stops any previous active music stream, opens a new BASS stream from already-read music bytes, and starts playback while caching the source path.

functions.name: stop_music_stream
functions.description: Stops the active BASS music stream, removes any registered sync callback, frees the stream handle, and clears the active-stream flag.

functions.name: load_registered_sound_sample_from_path
functions.description: Loads one sample directly from a filesystem path into the shared runtime sound-bank slot table via `BASS_SampleLoad(mem=0, ...)`, storing the resulting sample handle at the requested registered-sample index.

functions.name: load_registered_sound_sample_from_bytes
functions.description: Loads one sample from already-read archive bytes into the shared runtime sound-bank slot table via `BASS_SampleLoad(mem=1, ...)`, storing the resulting sample handle at the requested registered-sample index.

functions.name: play_registered_sound_sample_scaled
functions.description: Plays one registered runtime sample through the BASS sample backend with the supplied normalized gain scale and the default extra playback parameters.

functions.name: stop_sound_sample_handle
functions.description: Forwards one raw runtime sample handle directly into `BASS_SampleStop`, underneath the registered-sample and warning-owned stop wrappers.

functions.name: stop_registered_sound_sample
functions.description: Stops playback for one registered runtime sample index by resolving it through the shared sound-bank table before forwarding into the lower audio backend.

functions.name: is_registered_sound_sample_playing
functions.description: Returns whether one registered runtime sample index is currently playing by querying the shared sound-bank table entry in the lower audio backend.

functions.name: play_registered_sound_sample_default
functions.description: Plays one registered runtime sample index through the default `BASS_SamplePlayEx` wrapper with the hard-coded playback parameters used by the warning-owned sample path.

functions.name: play_registered_sound_sample_backend
functions.description: Forwards one registered runtime sample index plus normalized playback scalars into the raw `BASS_SamplePlayEx` lane that the SFX and voice wrappers share.

functions.name: play_registered_sound_sample_scaled_panned
functions.description: Plays one registered runtime sample through the BASS sample backend with the supplied normalized gain scale and explicit pan override.

functions.name: set_global_sample_volume_config
functions.description: Scales one normalized [0,1] sample-volume scalar onto BASS config option `4` (`BASS_CONFIG_GVOL_SAMPLE`) before the backend stores it as the live global SFX sample volume.

functions.name: set_global_stream_volume_config
functions.description: Scales one normalized [0,1] stream-volume scalar onto BASS config option `5` (`BASS_CONFIG_GVOL_STREAM`) before the backend stores it as the live music or stream volume.

functions.name: stop_audio_backend
functions.description: Forwards directly into the global BASS stop export to halt active backend playback during shutdown or full audio resets.

functions.name: resume_audio_backend_if_paused
functions.description: Restarts the global BASS backend only when the audio state flag at `backend + 0x18` says the backend is currently paused, then clears that paused flag.

functions.name: pause_audio_backend_if_running
functions.description: Pauses the global BASS backend only when the audio state flag at `backend + 0x18` says the backend is currently running, then marks it paused.

functions.name: set_audio_normalization_scales
functions.description: Stores the normalized music, SFX, and voice gain scales parsed from `VOICE/_VOICE.TXT` into the shared backend lanes that the music, sound, and voice wrappers multiply into live playback volume.

functions.name: initialize_font_wave_state
functions.description: Seeds the shared sine-wave text animation phases and per-tick phase increments that `draw_font_text_instance` consumes for wobble text.

functions.name: update_font_wave_state
functions.description: Advances the shared sine-wave text animation phases, wraps them into `[0, 2pi)`, and resets the transient font-wave bookkeeping lanes each frame.

functions.name: font_slot_index_for_char
functions.description: Maps front-end text bytes onto FONT-MENU-HOVER atlas slot indices; shipped tutorial strings reuse bytes like `{`, `}`, `[`, `]`, `~`, `_`, `^`, and `;` as icon tokens.

functions.name: measure_font_text_width
functions.description: Accumulates atlas slot widths for one string using the registered font's spacing scalar and trailing space padding.

functions.name: register_font_texture_sheet
functions.description: Loads one FONT-MENU-HOVER atlas, scans row-0 white delimiters and the column-0 height marker, and registers per-slot metrics and texture refs.

functions.name: draw_font_text_instance
functions.description: Draws one queued font text instance by mapping each byte through the atlas slot table and submitting textured glyph quads.

functions.name: draw_queued_font_quad_instance
functions.description: Draws one queued explicit font-quad record by forwarding its stored coordinates, UVs, size mode, angle, and color into the shared immediate textured-quad helper.

functions.name: draw_font_text_queue
functions.description: Iterates the live font text queue, retires dead entries, and draws the ones matching the supplied render mask.

functions.name: queue_font_text_instance
functions.description: Copies one text run into the shared font print string buffer and appends the matching queued font-text instance record with its font, position, alignment, colors, and render flags.

functions.name: queue_axis_aligned_textured_quad
functions.description: Appends one axis-aligned textured quad with default `[0,1]` UVs into the shared 2D render queue, using the supplied texture, authored rectangle, tint, and render flags.

functions.name: queue_axis_aligned_textured_quad_uv
functions.description: Appends one axis-aligned textured quad with explicit UV bounds into the shared 2D render queue. Front-end widgets, progress bars, and the damage gauge all use this helper.

functions.name: queue_textured_quad_corners
functions.description: Appends one arbitrary four-corner textured quad with explicit per-corner positions and UV bounds into the shared 2D render queue. The backdrop and galaxy-line renderers use this helper.

functions.name: layout_and_queue_wrapped_font_text
functions.description: Splits one text string into wrapped line runs, queues each run through `queue_font_text_instance`, and returns the aligned authored-space origin and measured bounds for the wrapped block.

functions.name: initialize_font3d_objects
functions.description: Builds the per-font `Objects/Font3D` bod instances, applies the atlas UV layout for each glyph slot, and scales each object to the registered font metrics.

functions.name: update_joystick_input
functions.description: Polls each enumerated DirectInput joystick device, reacquires on transient loss, normalizes its primary X/Y axes into the shared [-1000, 1000] range, packs the sampled buttons into the game's bitmask format, and publishes the result through the input-controller slot writer.

functions.name: update_keyboard_input
functions.description: Polls the DirectInput keyboard device into the current and previous 0x100-byte key-state tables, retries reacquisition on transient failures, and publishes the derived digital input flags and directional axes into the shared input-controller lanes.

functions.name: is_key_pressed_edge
functions.description: Returns true when the supplied keyboard scancode is down in the current DirectInput key-state table and was up in the previous frame's table.

functions.name: is_key_down
functions.description: Returns true when the supplied keyboard scancode is down in the current DirectInput key-state table.

functions.name: initialize_mouse_authored_scale_from_clip_rect
functions.description: Caches the current clipped screen rectangle and initializes the shared mouse screen-to-authored scaling for the default 640x480 authored coordinate space.

functions.name: update_mouse_authored_scale
functions.description: Computes the shared mouse screen-to-authored X/Y scale factors from the cached clip rectangle and the supplied authored width and height. convert_mouse_screen_xy consumes those scales.

functions.name: resolve_uncaptured_cursor_sensitivity_scale
functions.description: Returns the cursor-delta sensitivity scale for uncaptured pointer mode: 1.0 while the cursor is captured, otherwise arg * 1.8 + 0.7.

functions.name: is_mouse_captured
functions.description: Returns the capture-active byte at the head of the shared mouse-pointer state block stored at Game + 0x290.

functions.name: capture_mouse_cursor
functions.description: Enters captured-mouse mode for the shared Game + 0x290 pointer state: snapshots the current authored cursor coordinates, warps controller 0 there through click_mouse_screen, and marks the capture byte active.

functions.name: release_mouse_cursor
functions.description: Leaves captured-mouse mode for the shared Game + 0x290 pointer state by restoring the saved authored cursor coordinates into the live lanes and clearing the capture byte.

functions.name: convert_math_type32_to_16
functions.description: Converts one float into the shared signed-16 gameplay math representation by scaling with `65536 / scale` and truncating. Android symbols match this helper to `MathType32to16(float, float)`, and both Android and Windows only xref it from the SubGoldy update path.

functions.name: convert_math_type16_to_32
functions.description: Converts one signed-16 gameplay math value back into float form by scaling with `scale / 65536`. Android symbols match this helper to `MathType16to32(short, float)`, and both Android and Windows only xref it from the SubGoldy update path.

functions.name: initialize_math_random_table
functions.description: Fills the shared 0x1fff-entry gameplay random table from the underlying linear-congruential generator and resets the ring index. Android symbols match this helper family to `gRMathRand2Init()`.

functions.name: next_math_random_value
functions.description: Advances the shared gameplay random-table index modulo 0x1fff and returns the next cached 15-bit value. Android symbols match this helper family to the shared `Rand()` / `gRMathRand2()` accessors.

functions.name: initialize_trigonometry_tables
functions.description: Builds the shared cosine and sine lookup tables used by the gameplay math wrappers during startup, then resets the companion random table state.

functions.name: set_math_random_seed
functions.description: Stores one caller-provided seed into the shared gameplay linear-congruential generator before the random table is consumed. Android symbols match this helper family to `RandSeed(int)`.

functions.name: cosine
functions.description: Computes one cosine value through the shared gameplay trigonometry wrapper. Android symbols match this helper to `Cos(float)`.

functions.name: sine
functions.description: Computes one sine value through the shared gameplay trigonometry wrapper. Android symbols match this helper to `Sin(float)`.

functions.name: arccosine
functions.description: Computes the arccosine of one float input. Android symbols match this helper to `ACos(float)`.

functions.name: atan2_positive
functions.description: Computes a two-argument arctangent with the result wrapped into the positive full-turn range. Android symbols match this helper to `ATan(float, float)`.

functions.name: square_root
functions.description: Computes the square root of one float input. Android symbols match this helper to `Sqrt(float)`.

functions.name: multiply_vector_by_matrix_copy
functions.description: Writes an out-of-place affine vector-by-matrix transform into a destination output. Android symbols match this helper to `tVector::operator*(const tMatrix&) const`.

functions.name: dot_vectors
functions.description: Computes the 3D dot product of two vectors. Android symbols match this helper to the static overload `tVector::Dot(const tVector&, const tVector&)`.

functions.name: dot_vector
functions.description: Computes the 3D dot product of one vector against another through the member-style overload `tVector::Dot(const tVector&)`.

functions.name: multiply_vector_by_matrix
functions.description: Applies one affine matrix to a vector in place, including translation. Android symbols match this helper to `tVector::Multiply(const tMatrix&)`.

functions.name: rotate_vector_by_matrix
functions.description: Applies only the 3x3 rotational basis of a matrix to a vector in place, leaving translation out. Android symbols match this helper to `tVector::Rotate(const tMatrix&)`.

functions.name: normalize_vector_from_source
functions.description: Copies a source vector and normalizes it in place. Android symbols match this helper to `tVector::Normalize(const tVector&)`.

functions.name: cross_vectors
functions.description: Computes the 3D cross product of two vectors into a destination output. Android symbols match this helper to `tVector::Cross(const tVector&, const tVector&)`.

functions.name: initialize_uniform_scale_matrix
functions.description: Initializes a matrix from one scalar by writing the same value across the xyz diagonal, zeroing the off-diagonal and translation slots, and setting homogeneous `w` to 1.0. Android symbols match this helper to the single-float `tMatrix::tMatrix(float)` constructor.

functions.name: multiply_matrices
functions.description: Core affine matrix multiply that writes `out = lhs * rhs` for the 3x3 basis and translated origin row. Android symbols match this helper to `tMatrix::Multiply(const tMatrix&, const tMatrix&)`.

functions.name: set_matrix_identity
functions.description: Resets one full 4x4 transform matrix to identity, including translation. Android symbols match this helper to `tMatrix::Identity()`.

functions.name: set_matrix_rotation_identity
functions.description: Resets the rotational basis and homogeneous row to identity while preserving the translation slots. Android symbols match this helper to `tMatrix::RotIdentity()`.

functions.name: invert_matrix_in_place
functions.description: Inverts one orthonormal transform matrix in place by transposing the basis and recomputing the negated translated origin. Android symbols match this helper to `tMatrix::Invert()`.

functions.name: invert_matrix_from_source
functions.description: Writes the inverse of a source orthonormal transform into a destination matrix. Android symbols match this helper to `tMatrix::Invert(const tMatrix&)`.

functions.name: initialize_quaternion_from_axis
